task.title: Migrate WelcomeScreen + LangPickerScreen + project-creation flows from azt_recorder into azt_collab_client / azt_collabd, and fix the silent failure of open_server_ui()
task.summary:
Two related changes: (1) Fix bug: azt_collab_client.open_server_ui() reports ok=True even when `python -m azt_collabd ui` exits immediately because azt_collabd isn't on the spawned subprocess's sys.path. Same root cause affects the loopback transport's daemon auto-spawn. (2) Redesign: pull project-picking / project-creating UX out of the recorder and into the shared collab layer so sister apps reuse it. The recorder shrinks to "hand me a project path, I'll open it."
Do step 1 first; it's small and unblocks testing the rest. Steps 2-5 are the redesign and should be planned before code lands.

progress.step1: open_server_ui spawn fixed; new azt_collab_client/_spawn.py; stderr captured; new 'spawn_exited' result; loopback _spawn_server mirrors PYTHONPATH injection. Recorder 1.23.0 → 1.23.1; client 0.8.0 → 0.8.1.
progress.step2:
LangPickerScreen + KV moved to azt_collab_client/ui/langpicker.py. New register_langpicker_kv(font_name, langtags_path); new translate.tr() wrapper for KV ``#:import``. Recorder 1.23.1 → 1.24.0; client 0.8.1 → 0.9.0.
Copy langtags_mini.json.gz from azt_recorder/ to azt_collab_client/ui/assets/. Sandbox could not execute `cp` on a binary file. Once moved, drop the explicit ``langtags_path=`` kwarg in main.py:build().
progress.step3:
Template download moved to daemon. New azt_collabd.projects.create_from_template (urllib + size cap + atomic os.replace + register), new POST /v1/projects/from_template, new client create_project_from_template (returns Project on success or (None, error_str) on failure — the tuple-fallback was added after a regression where old daemons surfaced as "could not download" with no reason). Default SILCAWL URL moved to azt_collabd/config.py default_template_url() with AZT_DEFAULT_TEMPLATE_URL env override. Recorder 1.24.0 → 1.25.0; client 0.9.0 → 0.10.0; daemon 0.8.0 → 0.9.0; MIN_SERVER_VERSION bumped (user later relaxed back to 0.8.0 for compat).
setup_from_nuke.sh updated to install dulwich (auto-publish regression: spawned daemon used recorder's venv but it didn't have dulwich). Document in docs that dulwich is a daemon dep.
progress.step4: Clone-from-URL popup moved to azt_collab_client/ui/popups.py (clone_url_popup); new sync clone_project() wrapper in client __init__. Recorder clone_dialog reduced to 10-line wrapper that forwards to existing _clone_via_server driver (kept because of auth-retry branch). Recorder 1.25.0 → 1.26.0; client 0.10.0 → 0.11.0. Daemon unchanged.
progress.step5:
ProjectPickerScreen + register_picker_kv added to azt_collab_client/ui/picker.py. Recorder ScreenManager block, KV, and class swapped (WelcomeScreen → ProjectPickerScreen, 'welcome' → 'picker'). go_welcome → go_picker. Recorder 1.26.0 → 1.27.0; client 0.11.0 → 0.12.0.
show_start_over / new_from_template / open_file / clone_dialog stayed in recorder — each owns recorder-specific state. Picker delegates to them via the host contract.
progress.step6:
Standalone UI now mounts the shared ProjectPickerScreen as its 'picker' screen. New azt-collab/theme.py mirrors recorder/theme.py so the picker's KV imports satisfy. Read-only ProjectsScreen class+KV removed; CollabUIApp implements the picker host contract (icon/subtitle/version_string + go_config/list_projects/load_lift/ open_file/show_start_over/clone_dialog). open_file/show_start_over are stubs (point user back to the recorder); clone_dialog runs through clone_project on a worker thread. Recorder 1.27.0 → 1.27.1 (changelog continuity; no recorder code change).
Two-copy theme.py is a maintenance smell. Plan-time question was whether to rename azt-collab/ to "shared" and source theme from there. Worth tracking.
progress.step7:
Drafted 2026-05-01, revised same day after user clarified the design intent: ONE picker (no in-process Kivy fallback for Kivy hosts) and the picker OWNS all create flows (open / clone / start-new), returning a project path to the caller regardless of whether the project pre-existed.
Step 7 adds `python -m azt_collabd projects` as a helper subprocess and a matching pick_project() in azt_collab_client. The recorder's in-process picker (ProjectPickerScreen + LangPickerScreen mounts, open_file, clone_dialog, show_start_over, new_from_template, _download_and_open, _pending_vernlang) all come out of the recorder; cold-start and Start over both call pick_project(). The settings helper subprocess loses its Projects tab.
Daemon-vs-client split: azt_collabd owns the helper subprocess (the Kivy app + CLI entry); azt_collab_client owns pick_project() (the call-it helper). Sister apps only ever import the client; they never reach into azt_collabd.
All four open questions resolved 2026-05-01 by user decisions: Option 1 BUNDLE for Android (Activity ships in step 7); recorder backgrounds during pick with modal overlay on desktop; cold start hides the recorder window until a project is loaded; picker errors via in-window modal overlay.
See <step7_picker_entrypoint> and <resolved_questions> below for the full spec.
progress.observations: - Both copies of azt_collab_client/ (recorder copy and azt-collab canonical) are linked at the filesystem level: edits to one propagate to the other. Single edit covers both. The XML's original "symlink" hint was correct; the cross-check verification step is automatically satisfied. - Verification commands cannot run from this sandbox (subprocess is seccomp-blocked). Live verification has been done by the user out-of-band after each step. - Recorder ``_download_and_open`` left in place for now (it still serves the arbitrary-URL clone-from-link flow that step 4 owns). - clean_template stays in recorder's load_lift; moving it would require duplicating lift.py into the daemon (out of scope).

task.repo_layout:
The AZT suite lives under /home/kentr/bin/AZT/. Two sibling git repos:
/home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt_recorder/ — the recorder app (this repo) main.py — single-file Kivy app + KV azt_collab_client/ — symlink to canonical client copy collab.py — recorder's wrapper around azt_collab_client (logging, translations)
/home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt-collab/ — the shared backend repo azt_collabd/ — daemon (HTTP loopback + Android ContentProvider) __main__.py — `python -m azt_collabd ui` ui/app.py — standalone Kivy settings UI azt_collab_client/ — canonical client copy examples/sister_app.py — runnable demo
Both use venv in /home/kentr/bin/AZT/env.
On branch `collabd` of azt_recorder, the symlinks azt_collabd and examples were removed (git status shows `D azt_collabd`, `M azt_collab_client`, etc.). The recorder's azt_collab_client/ is a symlink. This was not understood earlier, but has been confirmed by the user; version drift should not be an issue.

step1_bugfix.title: Fix open_server_ui() silent failure (and matching auto-spawn bug)
step1_bugfix.root_cause:
open_server_ui() at azt_collab_client/__init__.py:28-67 spawns `[sys.executable, '-m', 'azt_collabd', 'ui']` with stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL and reports ok=True purely on Popen succeeding. When the recorder runs from /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt_recorder/ without a symlink to azt_collabd, that subprocess exits 1 with "No module named azt_collabd" — but stderr is swallowed and the caller logs "Opened sync settings." (main.py:3207). User clicks the button, sees the success log, nothing actually appears.
Same shape in transports/loopback.py:121-157 (_spawn_server) — the daemon auto-spawn fires `python -m azt_collabd` the same way, with the same suppressed stderr, but at least there the post-spawn health-poll loop (lines 148-156) catches the failure by timing out; caller eventually sees ServerUnavailable. Still worth fixing so the timeout isn't the only signal.
Android is unaffected: - open_server_ui() short-circuits at the top with `return {'ok': False, 'error': 'desktop_only'}` for platform == 'android'. - On Android the client uses the ContentProvider transport (transports/android_cp.py), not loopback, so _spawn_server is never reached.

fix.change:
Add a small private helper that locates azt_collabd:
def _locate_azt_collabd_parent(): """Return a directory that, when added to PYTHONPATH, makes `import azt_collabd` work — or '' if azt_collabd is already importable (or genuinely can't be found).""" try: import azt_collabd # noqa: F401 return '' except ImportError: pass import os here = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)) # azt_collab_client lives under either: # .../azt-collab/azt_collab_client/ (canonical) # ...//azt_collab_client/ (copy) # Try sibling-of-grandparent .../azt-collab/azt_collabd for candidate in ( os.path.dirname(here), # parent of azt_collab_client os.path.join(os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(here)), 'azt-collab'), # sibling-named azt-collab dir ): if os.path.isdir(os.path.join(candidate, 'azt_collabd')): return candidate return ''
Update open_server_ui(): - Build env: copy os.environ, prepend _locate_azt_collabd_parent() to PYTHONPATH if non-empty. - After Popen, poll proc.poll() for ~250 ms. If it returned a non-zero code, return {'ok': False, 'error': 'spawn_exited', 'returncode': rc}. Otherwise {'ok': True, 'pid': proc.pid}. - Pipe stderr to PIPE so we can include the first ~200 bytes in the error dict on failure (not on success — keep it cheap).
On the recorder side (main.py:3198-3216 open_server_ui), the existing 'desktop_only' / 'server_apk_not_installed' branches stay; add a new 'spawn_exited' branch that surfaces a sensible message, e.g. "Sync settings UI failed to start: {detail}" via _set_log.
Mirror the PYTHONPATH injection in _spawn_server (lines 121-157) using the same helper. The post-spawn health-poll loop already handles the failure mode, but consistency is worth it.
fix.verification: - From azt_recorder/: `env/bin/python -c "from azt_collab_client import open_server_ui; print(open_server_ui())"` should print {'ok': True, 'pid': N} AND a Kivy settings window should open. - Then kill it and rerun without azt_collabd findable (e.g. temporarily move ../azt-collab/azt_collabd aside) — should print {'ok': False, 'error': 'spawn_exited', ...}. - Recorder UI: tap "Open sync settings" on welcome's gear page; settings UI window should appear, log says "Opened sync settings." On a deliberately-broken setup the log should say something better than the current silent success.
fix.do_not: - Do NOT remove the 'desktop_only' early return. - Do NOT change the Android ContentProvider transport. - Do NOT add any user-visible Python traceback; failure path is a translatable user-facing string.

step2_redesign.title: Move project-picking UX from recorder into azt_collab_client + azt_collabd
step2_redesign.motivation:
"Pick or start a wordlist" is a project-registry operation. The registry already lives server-side (azt_collabd/projects.py, projects.json, register_project, list_projects). The recorder's WelcomeScreen and LangPickerScreen and the "Start New" / clone / open-file flows are duplicated work that every sister app would otherwise re-implement. Move them once into the shared layer.
The recorder ends up as: "give me a project path, I'll open it with my own LIFTDatabase loader." Sister apps mount the same picker and supply their own loader.
step2_redesign.current_recorder_inventory:
Lines below are from /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt_recorder/main.py at the time this XML was written (commit f76210e on `collabd`). They will drift; treat as starting points, then re-grep.
KV definitions: - WelcomeScreen KV : main.py:214 (registration), main.py:230 (block) - LangPickerScreen : main.py:222 (registration), main.py:1060 (block)
Python classes: - class WelcomeScreen(Screen) : main.py:1406 - class LangPickerScreen(Screen) : main.py:1431 (uses LangPickerScreen._langtags class-level cache)
Recorder methods that drive the picker actions: - open_file() : opens native file chooser (Plyer / Android Intent), returns LIFT path - clone_dialog() : URL prompt, then clone - show_start_over() : main.py:5009 — popup: "Start a new wordlist with this template?" - new_from_template() : main.py:4266 - _download_and_open(url) : downloads SILCAWL.zip, unpacks, calls load_lift - _SILCAWL_URL : template URL constant - _pending_vernlang : transient state set by LangPickerScreen, consumed by load_lift() at main.py:4712-4716
Recorder methods that stay recorder-only: - load_lift(path) : LIFTDatabase + queue setup — recorder-specific consumer of a chosen path - go_config() : recorder settings gear - everything inside RecorderScreen and below
Recorder methods that already delegate correctly: - open_server_ui (main.py:3198) : already routes through client; keep as-is after step 1's fix - do_sync, _auto_commit_sync, etc. : already client-driven
step2_redesign.target_layout:
NEW module: /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt-collab/azt_collab_client/ui/ __init__.py — public exports picker.py — ProjectPickerScreen (KV + class) langpicker.py — LangPickerScreen (KV + class) popups.py — start_from_template_popup, clone URL prompt, etc. assets/langtags.json — moved from azt_recorder/
NEW client API in azt_collab_client/__init__.py: - open_project_from_path(path) -> Project Wraps register_project(); returns the Project record. The host app provides `path` from its native file picker. - clone_project(url, dest_dir, host) -> Project Routes through daemon (already mostly there); rename for symmetry with the other two. - create_project_from_template(template_url, vernlang, dest_dir) -> Project Daemon downloads, unpacks, runs clean_template, registers.
NEW daemon endpoint(s) for the template download (so the daemon owns network + disk for it): POST /v1/projects/from_template body: {template_url, vernlang, dest_dir} returns: {ok, project: {...}} or {ok: False, error}
KV registration (host app does this once): from azt_collab_client.ui import ProjectPickerScreen ScreenManager: ProjectPickerScreen: name: 'picker' on_project_ready: app.on_project_ready(*args) LangPickerScreen: name: 'langpicker' # owned by client, mounted by host # ... host's own screens follow
Host callbacks the picker requires: - request_file_path() -> str | None Host pops its native file chooser; picker calls this when the "Open from disk" action fires. Recorder wires it to its existing Plyer/Android-Intent code. - on_project_ready(project_path, project) -> None Host receives the chosen project; for the recorder this is essentially today's load_lift(project_path) + sm.current = 'recorder'.
Wiring pattern (host side): picker = self.root.ids.picker picker.request_file_path = self._native_file_picker picker.bind(on_project_ready=self._on_project_ready)
step2_redesign.function_relocation_table:
Nothing is being deleted from the ecosystem — every function moves to a new home. This table is the contract.
| Recorder, today | New home | |-----------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------| | WelcomeScreen KV (main.py:230) | azt_collab_client/ui/picker.py KV | | class WelcomeScreen (main.py:1406) | azt_collab_client/ui/picker.py — ProjectPickerScreen | | LangPickerScreen KV (main.py:1060) | azt_collab_client/ui/langpicker.py KV | | class LangPickerScreen (main.py:1431) | azt_collab_client/ui/langpicker.py | | langtags asset | azt_collab_client/ui/assets/langtags.json | | open_file() | host-supplied request_file_path() callback + open_project_from_path() | | clone_dialog() | URL-prompt UI in picker.py + azt_collab_client.clone_project() | | show_start_over() popup | azt_collab_client/ui/popups.py — start_from_template_popup() | | new_from_template() | azt_collab_client.create_project_from_template() | | _download_and_open(url) | daemon: azt_collabd.projects.create_from_template() | | _SILCAWL_URL | daemon config (azt_collabd/config.py) or a default in the client API | | clean_template logic | daemon: azt_collabd.projects (alongside create_from_template) | | _pending_vernlang plumbing | internal state of ProjectPickerScreen; persisted on Project record | | existing-projects list rendering | built into ProjectPickerScreen, driven by list_projects() |
Stays recorder-only (genuinely recorder-specific, not migrated): - load_lift(path) and everything it calls (LIFTDatabase, RecorderController, queue rebuild, prefs, image cache). - The gear button → go_config() → recorder settings page. - The recorder's version label. - Everything inside RecorderScreen.
step2_redesign.migration_order:
Land each step as its own commit; verify the recorder still works between every step. Bump azt_collab_client.__version__ minor at the end of step 2; bump major when step 5 lands and the recorder no longer owns any picker code.
1. Step 1 bugfix (open_server_ui spawn) — already specified above; lands first because it's needed to test the new picker UI from the standalone azt_collabd ui app.
2. Move LangPickerScreen + langtags asset into azt_collab_client/ui/langpicker.py. Recorder still owns WelcomeScreen but imports LangPickerScreen from the client and registers it in its ScreenManager. _pending_vernlang stays in the recorder for now (consumed by load_lift). Verify: language flow still works end-to-end on desktop; no behavior change.
3. Move template-download to the daemon: - Add azt_collabd.projects.create_from_template(url, vernlang, dest_dir, contributor) returning Project. - Add POST /v1/projects/from_template endpoint. - Add azt_collab_client.create_project_from_template(). - Recorder's new_from_template() becomes a thin wrapper that calls the client API and then load_lift(). - SILCAWL URL moves to azt_collabd/config.py default. Verify: "Start New" still produces a working LIFT.
4. Move clone path: - Promote clone_project() to a public client API. - The URL-prompt popup moves to ui/popups.py (callable from recorder for now). Verify: clone-from-URL produces a working LIFT.
5. Introduce ProjectPickerScreen in azt_collab_client/ui/picker.py. Wire it into the recorder as a replacement for WelcomeScreen: - Recorder's main.py imports ProjectPickerScreen, registers it in the KV ScreenManager as name='picker', and removes its WelcomeScreen KV+class. - Host callbacks: request_file_path (recorder's existing native picker), on_project_ready (load_lift + transition to 'recorder' screen). - Recorder deletes show_start_over, new_from_template (now just inside picker), open_file (now just inside picker), clone_dialog (now just inside picker). - _pending_vernlang plumbing collapses into picker internals. Verify: full welcome → pick / create / clone → recorder loop on desktop and on Android (real device or emulator).
6. Reuse picker in standalone server UI: - azt_collabd/ui/app.py adds the same ProjectPickerScreen as its initial screen (replacing or augmenting today's read-only "projects" tab). Validates that the picker is host-app-agnostic.
step2_redesign.android_considerations: - Native file picker stays host-side: each app provides its own request_file_path() because the Activity context is host-owned. The picker UI calls the callback; it doesn't try to do anything platform-specific itself. - The picker UI must not import anything from azt_collabd directly (the README convention: "client is free of any azt_collabd dependency", azt_collab_client/paths.py:2). All daemon ops go through the client RPC layer. - Suite signing requirement (android/SUITE_FINGERPRINT) is unchanged — the picker doesn't broaden the ContentProvider surface, just calls existing endpoints. - On Android the daemon runs in-process under the host APK; the picker calls list_projects()/register_project() via the ContentProvider transport. No subprocess.
step2_redesign.i18n: All user-visible strings inside azt_collab_client/ui/* must use the client's translator hook (azt_collab_client.translate.set_translator pattern), not hard-code English. The recorder's existing _tr() can be registered as the translator at startup. README convention: "The backend has no Kivy and no i18n imports. UI marshaling and translation are the host app's job." — but this UI module IS Kivy, so the rule there is: pull translations through set_translator, don't import gettext directly. Host strings and translations from this UI here, so they can be one source of truth, and sourced by clients.
step2_redesign.styling: Use the existing theme module (recorder's theme.py), which should now be hosted in azt-collab (which should probably be renamed to `shared`, and sourced by clients from there.
step2_redesign.do_not: - Do NOT delete any function before its replacement is wired up and verified — every step in the migration order must keep the recorder runnable. - Do NOT couple the picker to LIFT specifically. It hands back a project path; whether the host treats that as a LIFT, a Toolbox file, etc. is the host's problem. - Do NOT introduce any synchronous network calls from the Kivy main thread; template download / clone go through the daemon and use request_sync / poll_job patterns where possible. - Do NOT change the existing project registry on-disk format without a migration. projects.json is shared with installed sister apps.

step7_picker_entrypoint.title: Replace the recorder's in-process picker with one shared helper subprocess (`python -m azt_collabd projects`) + pick_project() client helper. The picker owns all create flows and returns a project path; the recorder shrinks to "given a path, load it."
step7_picker_entrypoint.motivation:
Today the recorder's "Start over" button (main.py:589-592) calls app.go_picker(), which navigates to the in-process ProjectPickerScreen embedded in the recorder's own ScreenManager. Cold start (no last_lift) lands on the same in-process picker because it's the first screen registered (main.py:217-218). That pattern locks every sister app to Kivy: a Qt or web-based recorder cannot mount Kivy Screens.
The settings helper subprocess (`python -m azt_collabd ui`) already runs in a separate process via open_server_ui() (azt_collab_client/__init__.py:28-82). That subprocess pattern is the only model that is toolkit-agnostic. Project picking should use the same pattern: launch one Kivy helper, let it print the chosen path on stdout, exit. Any host (Kivy, Qt, curses, web) calls it the same way.
Hard rule, set by the user: ONE picker. There is no in-process Kivy fallback for "Kivy clients" alongside a subprocess for "non-Kivy clients" — that's two implementations of the same thing. Both paths invoke the helper subprocess via pick_project(). The recorder no longer mounts ProjectPickerScreen in its own ScreenManager.
Hard rule, also from the user: the picker owns all create flows. Tapping "I have one on my phone" opens a native file chooser inside the picker; "Clone Internet Repository" runs the URL-prompt + daemon clone inside the picker; "Start New" runs the language picker + daemon create-from-template inside the picker. Whether the project pre-existed or was just created, the picker hands the host one path. The host loads it.
Consequences for the recorder: open_file(), clone_dialog(), show_start_over(), new_from_template(), _download_and_open(), _pending_vernlang, the LangPickerScreen mount, and the ProjectPickerScreen mount all leave the recorder. The recorder ends up as: "given a path, load it." That's the goal originally stated in step2_redesign <motivation>; step 5 was a stepping stone that left those methods in the recorder because the picker was still embedded.
The settings helper subprocess also has a Projects tab today (azt_collabd/ui/app.py SettingsScreen NavBar → 'picker' screen) whose load_lift is a stub (app.py:456-464); tapping a project just shows "Open it in the recorder to edit." That dead-end goes away — picking moves to its own entry point; settings shrinks to credentials + host toggle.
step7_picker_entrypoint.files:
Server (azt_collabd) — owns the helper subprocess: /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt-collab/azt_collabd/__main__.py /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt-collab/azt_collabd/ui/picker_app.py (new) /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt-collab/azt_collabd/ui/app.py /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt-collab/azt_collabd/__init__.py (version bump)
Client (azt_collab_client) — owns the call-it-from-anywhere helper: /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt-collab/azt_collab_client/__init__.py /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt-collab/azt_collab_client/ui/picker.py (hide_settings_gear flag) /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt-collab/azt_collab_client/ui/langpicker.py (host-callback hook if needed)
Recorder (the host that consumes pick_project): /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt_recorder/main.py /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt_recorder/CHANGELOG.txt /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt_recorder/claud_diffs.txt /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt_recorder/setup_from_nuke.sh (only if build steps change)
Cross-toolkit example: /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt-collab/examples/non_kivy_pick.py (new)
Changelog (azt-collab): /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt-collab/CHANGELOG.md
step7_picker_entrypoint.split_summary:
Quick reference for the daemon-vs-client split (more detail in <design> below):
azt_collabd │ azt_collab_client ───────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────── __main__.py: + 'projects' │ __init__.py: + pick_project() subcommand │ subprocess.Popen wrapper + ui/picker_app.py: NEW. The │ AZT_PICK stdout parser Kivy app the subprocess │ ui/picker.py: + hide_settings_gear actually runs. Hosts shared │ flag (no logic change to the screens, implements all │ screen class itself) host-contract callbacks for │ ui/langpicker.py: existing existing/clone/start-new. │ on-vernlang callback hook (or ui/app.py: drop Projects │ slight tweak so picker_app can tab + corresponding stubs │ subscribe). Screen class from CollabUIApp. │ unchanged.
Sister apps (recorder, future Qt/etc.) only ever import azt_collab_client. They never reach into azt_collabd.

design.entry_points: `python -m azt_collabd ui` — settings: credentials, host toggle, GitHub/GitLab connect screens. Existing; scope-narrowed in this step (no more Projects tab). `python -m azt_collabd projects` — minimal Kivy app: shows ProjectPickerScreen full-screen. On project tap, prints the LIFT path on stdout (single sentinel line, no extra logging) and exits 0. On cancel / window-close, exits 1 with empty stdout.
design.stdout_protocol:
Sentinel-prefixed line, parsing-resilient against Kivy log chatter that may leak to stdout in some configurations:
AZT_PICK\t<absolute-lift-path>\n
picker_app responsibilities: - Configure Kivy logger to write to stderr only (KIVY_NO_CONSOLELOG env or Logger handler swap before App.run; KIVY_NO_FILELOG=1 to skip the rolling log too). - On selection: sys.stdout.write(f'AZT_PICK\\t{path}\\n') then sys.stdout.flush() then App.stop(). - On window-close without selection: App.stop() with a non-zero process exit code (set a class-level self._exit_code = 1 by default, flip to 0 on success; sys.exit(self._exit_code) at the bottom of main()).
Client side (pick_project): scan stdout lines for the AZT_PICK\t prefix, take the first match, ignore everything else. Empty / no match with rc != 0 → cancelled.
design.client_api:
New in azt_collab_client/__init__.py:
def pick_project(timeout_seconds: int | None = None) -> dict: """Launch the standalone project-picker UI as a subprocess and return the selected project. Blocks until the picker window closes.
Returns: {'ok': True, 'path': '/abs/path/to/file.lift'} on selection. {'ok': False, 'error': 'cancelled'} if user closed window without picking. {'ok': False, 'error': 'desktop_only'} on Android (until the server APK grows a picker Activity — see <android> below). {'ok': False, 'error': 'spawn_exited', 'returncode': N, 'detail': '...'} if the subprocess crashed before the user could interact (e.g. azt_collabd not importable). {'ok': False, 'error': 'timeout'} if timeout_seconds elapsed. """
Implementation mirrors open_server_ui(): same _spawn helper for PYTHONPATH injection (step 1's fix), but uses subprocess.Popen with stdout=PIPE and a blocking communicate(timeout=...). Parse stdout for the AZT_PICK line.
Add 'pick_project' to __all__.
design.picker_app_module:
New file: azt-collab/azt_collabd/ui/picker_app.py
This is the picker. It hosts the shared screens already in azt_collab_client.ui (ProjectPickerScreen + LangPickerScreen) and implements every host-contract callback the picker exposes — including the three create-flow buttons. Every successful path through the UI converges on writing AZT_PICK\\t<path> and exiting 0.
Class-level skeleton (final code is the implementer's; this sets the contract):
""" Picker helper subprocess for `python -m azt_collabd projects`. Hosts ProjectPickerScreen + LangPickerScreen. All create flows (open / clone / start-new) run inside this process and return their resulting path through the same exit channel. """ import os, sys os.environ.setdefault('KIVY_NO_CONSOLELOG', '1')
from kivy.app import App from kivy.clock import Clock from kivy.lang import Builder from kivy.uix.screenmanager import ScreenManager, SlideTransition from kivy.properties import StringProperty
import azt_collab_client from azt_collab_client import ( list_projects, register_project, clone_project, create_project_from_template, ) from azt_collab_client.ui import ( ProjectPickerScreen, LangPickerScreen, register_picker_kv, register_langpicker_kv, clone_url_popup, theme, )
_AZT_ICON = os.path.join( os.path.dirname(azt_collab_client.__file__), 'azt.png')
KV = ''' <_PickerRoot>: ProjectPickerScreen: name: 'picker' LangPickerScreen: name: 'langpicker' '''
class _PickerRoot(ScreenManager): pass
class PickerApp(App): title = 'A-Z+T — Pick a project' subtitle = StringProperty('Pick a project') icon = StringProperty(_AZT_ICON) version_string = StringProperty('') _exit_code = 1 # default: cancelled
# ── Lifecycle ───────────────────────────────────── def build(self): theme.set_theme('Ocean') Builder.load_string(KV) register_picker_kv(font_name='Roboto') register_langpicker_kv( font_name='Roboto', langtags_path=os.path.join( os.path.dirname(azt_collab_client.__file__), 'ui', 'assets', 'langtags.json.gz'), ) self.sm = _PickerRoot(transition=SlideTransition()) return self.sm
# ── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────── def _emit_and_quit(self, path): sys.stdout.write(f'AZT_PICK\\t{path}\\n') sys.stdout.flush() self._exit_code = 0 self.stop()
def _show_error(self, msg): """Modal overlay inside the picker window. Window stays open after dismiss; user can retry or close.""" from kivy.uix.modalview import ModalView from kivy.uix.boxlayout import BoxLayout from kivy.uix.label import Label from kivy.uix.button import Button view = ModalView(size_hint=(0.85, None), height=180, auto_dismiss=False) box = BoxLayout(orientation='vertical', padding=12, spacing=10) box.add_widget(Label(text=msg)) btn = Button(text='Dismiss', size_hint_y=None, height=44) btn.bind(on_release=view.dismiss) box.add_widget(btn) view.add_widget(box) view.open()
# ── ProjectPickerScreen host contract ───────────── def list_projects(self): try: ps = list_projects() or [] except Exception: ps = [] return [(p.langcode, p.lift_path or p.working_dir) for p in ps]
def load_lift(self, path): """Existing-project tap → emit the path and exit.""" self._emit_and_quit(path)
def go_config(self): # The picker has no settings gear of its own. The # gear icon in the shared picker KV either hides # here or no-ops. Decision: hide via a flag passed # to register_picker_kv (see <ui_changes> below). pass
# ── Create flow: "I have one on my phone" ──────── def open_file(self): """Native file chooser → register with daemon → emit path.""" from plyer import filechooser # lazy def _on_chosen(selection): if not selection: return # user cancelled chooser, stay on picker path = selection[0] try: # register with daemon so it appears in the # registry next time register_project(lift_path=path) except Exception: pass # registration is best-effort Clock.schedule_once( lambda dt: self._emit_and_quit(path), 0) filechooser.open_file( title='Pick a .lift file', filters=[['LIFT', '*.lift']], on_selection=_on_chosen, )
# ── Create flow: "Clone Internet Repository" ────── def clone_dialog(self): """URL prompt → daemon clone → emit cloned path.""" def _on_submit(url): import threading def _worker(): try: import azt_collabd dest = os.path.join( azt_collabd.paths.azt_home(), 'projects', url.rstrip('/').split('/')[-1] .replace('.git', '')) resp = clone_project(url, dest) except Exception as ex: Clock.schedule_once( lambda dt: self._show_error( f'Clone failed: {ex}'), 0) return if resp.get('ok') and resp.get('lift_path'): Clock.schedule_once( lambda dt: self._emit_and_quit( resp['lift_path']), 0) else: Clock.schedule_once( lambda dt: self._show_error( f'Clone failed: ' f'{resp.get("error", "?")}'), 0) threading.Thread( target=_worker, daemon=True).start() clone_url_popup(_on_submit)
# ── Create flow: "Start New" ────────────────────── def show_start_over(self): """Navigate to LangPickerScreen; the picker reads app._pending_vernlang on return and dispatches to create_project_from_template.""" self._pending_vernlang = '' self.sm.current = 'langpicker'
def on_vernlang_chosen(self, vernlang): """LangPickerScreen calls this once the user picks a language. Drives the daemon's template download and emits the new project's lift path.""" self._pending_vernlang = vernlang import threading, azt_collabd def _worker(): dest_dir = os.path.join( azt_collabd.paths.azt_home(), 'projects', vernlang) try: ret = create_project_from_template( vernlang=vernlang, dest_dir=dest_dir) except Exception as ex: Clock.schedule_once( lambda dt: self._show_error( f'Template download failed: {ex}'), 0) return project, err = (ret if isinstance(ret, tuple) else (ret, '')) if project and project.lift_path: Clock.schedule_once( lambda dt: self._emit_and_quit( project.lift_path), 0) else: Clock.schedule_once( lambda dt: self._show_error( f'Could not create: ' f'{err or "unknown"}'), 0) threading.Thread( target=_worker, daemon=True).start()
def main(): rc = PickerApp().run() sys.exit(PickerApp._exit_code if rc is None else rc)
design.ui_changes:
Two small additions to the shared client UI in azt_collab_client/ui/, both behind feature flags so existing callers (already-shipped sister apps) continue to work unchanged:
1. picker.register_kv() / register_picker_kv() grows a `hide_settings_gear: bool = False` kwarg. When True the gear button at the top of ProjectPickerScreen is omitted (the picker_app uses this — it has no settings of its own).
2. langpicker has, or grows, an `on_vernlang` callback hook so PickerApp.on_vernlang_chosen() runs when a language is selected. If the existing LangPickerScreen already writes to a host-supplied attribute (today's recorder reads app._pending_vernlang on return), simplest path is to keep that contract: PickerApp sets _pending_vernlang and the screen calls back via app.on_vernlang_chosen() (or app.load_lift() routed through PickerApp's override). Implementer to pick whichever the existing screen makes cleanest.
design.settings_ui_changes:
In azt-collab/azt_collabd/ui/app.py: - Drop the "Projects" Button in SettingsScreen NavBar (currently lines 147-149) and the ProjectPickerScreen registration in <RootSM> (line 60). - Drop list_projects / load_lift / open_file / show_start_over / clone_dialog stubs from CollabUIApp (lines 446-509) — they existed only to satisfy the picker host contract; with the picker no longer mounted here they're unused. - Drop the ProjectPickerScreen and clone_url_popup imports (line 46-47) and the clone_project, list_projects imports (lines 35-45) that become unused. - Settings UI is now strictly credentials + host. Smaller, no dead-end paths.
Wire the azt.png icon into the (now slimmer) settings UI here too: `icon = StringProperty(_AZT_ICON)` with the same os.path.join-from-azt_collab_client resolution as picker_app. Both standalone Kivy apps share the suite icon.
design.recorder_changes:
The recorder gives up its in-process picker entirely. All of the following come out of /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt_recorder/main.py:
Removals (verify each with grep before deleting; line numbers will drift):
KV / ScreenManager: - ProjectPickerScreen registration in <RootSM> (main.py:217-218: `ProjectPickerScreen: name: 'picker'`) - LangPickerScreen registration (main.py:225-226) - First-screen position so the recorder no longer "starts on" the picker — see cold-start path below
Imports: - `from azt_collab_client.ui import (ProjectPickerScreen, LangPickerScreen, register_picker_kv, register_langpicker_kv, ...)` line near main.py:24-27; keep only what's still used (theme + clone_url_popup if referenced elsewhere; otherwise drop the whole block). - `register_picker_kv(...)` and `register_langpicker_kv(...)` calls in build() (main.py:3436 and surrounding).
Methods on LIFTRecorderApp: - open_file() (main.py: ~) - clone_dialog() (main.py: ~) - show_start_over() (main.py:4489) - new_from_template() (main.py:3714) - _download_and_open() (main.py: ~) - go_picker() (main.py:4537) [no other caller after start_over and cold-start move to pick_project; verify with grep before deleting] - The _pending_vernlang attribute and any code reading or writing it (main.py:3719, ~4712-4716, etc.) - _SILCAWL_URL constant if unused after _download_and_open goes - Any "Start over" popup helpers (BoxLayout/Label construction inside show_start_over()) — gone with the method
KV button rewires: - "Start over" RecBtn (main.py:589-592): on_release: app.start_over()
Additions:
1. New method on LIFTRecorderApp:
def start_over(self): """Commit/sync, gray-out the recorder, spawn the picker, then either load the chosen LIFT or restore the recorder window.""" self._auto_commit_sync() self._show_pick_overlay() import threading from azt_collab_client import pick_project def _worker(): result = pick_project() Clock.schedule_once( lambda dt: self._handle_pick(result), 0) threading.Thread( target=_worker, daemon=True).start()
def _show_pick_overlay(self): """Modal overlay that blocks input on the recorder window while the picker is open.""" from kivy.uix.modalview import ModalView from kivy.uix.label import Label self._pick_overlay = ModalView( size_hint=(0.6, None), height=140, auto_dismiss=False, background_color=theme.OVERLAY_DARK) self._pick_overlay.add_widget(Label( text=_tr('Pick a project to continue.'))) self._pick_overlay.open()
def _hide_pick_overlay(self): if getattr(self, '_pick_overlay', None): self._pick_overlay.dismiss() self._pick_overlay = None
def _handle_pick(self, result): self._hide_pick_overlay() if result.get('ok'): self.load_lift(result['path']) return err = result.get('error', 'unknown') if err == 'cancelled': return # user backed out; recorder stays put if err == 'server_apk_not_installed': self._prompt_install_server_apk() return self._set_log(_tr( 'Could not open project picker: {error}') .format(error=err))
2. Cold-start path. Today the ScreenManager's first child is ProjectPickerScreen, so a fresh launch with no last_lift lands the user on the picker. After this step:
User constraint: do NOT show the recorder window first with the picker layered on top. Pick first, then bring up the recorder. The recorder window does not appear until a project is loaded.
Desktop sequencing (recorder __main__ / on_start): - Read prefs.last_lift before App.run(). - If present and readable: build the App normally, load_lift on first frame, recorder window appears already populated. (Same as today.) - If absent / missing: call pick_project() SYNCHRONOUSLY before App.run(). The picker is the only visible window. On result: * ok: call load_lift on the returned path, THEN App.run(). * cancelled: exit the recorder process cleanly (sys.exit(0)). The user closing the picker on cold start = "I don't want to use the recorder right now." * spawn_exited / other error: print to stderr, exit non-zero.
Android sequencing (cold start): - Same logic, but the "synchronously call pick_project()" pre-App.run() phase doesn't translate cleanly because the recorder's Activity is the entry point. Instead: * Recorder Activity starts, immediately fires the PICK_PROJECT Intent via startActivityForResult. The PickerActivity in the server APK comes foreground; the recorder Activity is paused behind it (standard Android lifecycle). * onActivityResult delivers the chosen path. On RESULT_OK call load_lift then proceed to the recorder UI; on RESULT_CANCELED finish() the recorder Activity (back-out from cold start = exit). * Implementer note: ensure no recorder UI frames render between Activity start and the picker coming foreground (use a splash or a black Theme.NoDisplay-style activity-alias for the gate Activity if needed; otherwise the standard splash already covers it).
Implementer note: a "no project loaded" placeholder state in RecorderScreen is no longer necessary — cancel on cold start exits, cancel during Start over keeps the previous project loaded.
3. Wherever the recorder previously navigated to 'picker' from elsewhere (search for `sm.current = 'picker'` and callers of go_picker()), replace with start_over() if the intent was "let the user switch", or remove if the intent was a no-op.
Result: main.py loses several hundred lines of code. The KV ScreenManager block shrinks to RecorderScreen / ConfigScreen / CollabScreen / ImagePickerScreen. The recorder's job is load_lift() and the recording UX.
design.android:
Subprocess fork is unavailable inside the Android app sandbox, so `python -m azt_collabd projects` is desktop-only as a literal command. The matching shape on Android is a new Activity inside the standalone server APK (org.atoznback.aztcollab) that sister apps launch via Intent; the chosen path comes back through onActivityResult. pick_project() on Android dispatches to that Activity instead of subprocess.Popen.
DECISION (user, 2026-05-01): Option 1 BUNDLE — Android Activity ships in step 7 alongside desktop, so the recorder's in-process picker is deleted unconditionally and the "one picker" rule holds on every platform from the moment step 7 lands. There is no step 8; everything lives in step 7.
Implementation outline. The server APK is already a python-for-android Kivy bundle (server_apk/buildozer.spec line 22 — `requirements = python3,kivy,pyjnius,...`; main.py line 62 launches CollabUIApp as the launcher Activity). So the Activity infrastructure exists; we add a second entry path for it.
1. Manifest declaration (server_apk/manifest_extras.xml or p4a_hook injection):
Add an <activity-alias> (or a second <activity> pointing to PythonActivity) with an intent-filter for action `org.atoznback.aztcollab.PICK_PROJECT` and category DEFAULT. This lets sister apps fire startActivityForResult(intent_with_that_action).
Top-level <permission> in manifest_extras.xml stays as-is. The new alias goes inside <application> and is therefore added by p4a_hook.py's injection step alongside the existing <provider> injection (server_apk/buildozer.spec lines 53-60 explain the split: top-level vs application-level injections live in different places).
2. Server APK entry-point dispatch (azt-collab/server_apk/main.py):
At startup, read the launching Intent action via pyjnius:
from jnius import autoclass PythonActivity = autoclass('org.kivy.android.PythonActivity') intent = PythonActivity.mActivity.getIntent() action = intent.getAction() if intent else None
Dispatch: - action == 'org.atoznback.aztcollab.PICK_PROJECT' → from azt_collabd.ui.picker_app import main as picker_main; picker_main() PickerApp's _emit_and_quit replaced on Android (see step 3 below) so it sets the Activity result instead of writing stdout. - else (LAUNCHER / no action) → existing azt_collabd.ui.app.main() (settings UI). No change to that path.
This keeps both UIs in the same PythonActivity, which is what python-for-android allows out of the box — multiple Activities require custom Java glue we don't want.
3. PickerApp Android adapter (in azt_collabd/ui/picker_app.py):
_emit_and_quit() needs a platform branch. On Android, instead of writing AZT_PICK to stdout, set the Activity result and finish:
if platform == 'android': from jnius import autoclass PythonActivity = autoclass( 'org.kivy.android.PythonActivity') Intent = autoclass('android.content.Intent') activity = PythonActivity.mActivity data = Intent() data.putExtra('path', path) activity.setResult(-1, data) # RESULT_OK = -1 activity.finish() else: sys.stdout.write(f'AZT_PICK\\t{path}\\n') sys.stdout.flush()
Cancel / window-close on Android: similarly call setResult(0) (RESULT_CANCELED) before finish(). The Kivy on_request_close hook fires when the user backs out of the Activity; wire it.
4. Client-side Android pick_project() (azt_collab_client/__init__.py and/or transports/android_cp.py):
Platform branch in pick_project():
from kivy.utils import platform if platform == 'android': return _pick_project_android() return _pick_project_desktop()
_pick_project_android() outline:
from jnius import autoclass, PythonJavaClass, java_method PythonActivity = autoclass( 'org.kivy.android.PythonActivity') Intent = autoclass('android.content.Intent')
intent = Intent('org.atoznback.aztcollab.PICK_PROJECT') # Use a callback-aware startActivityForResult shim; # android.activity (kivy.utils-shipped helper) supports # bind(on_activity_result=...) for this exact pattern. from android import activity as android_activity
result_holder = {'done': False, 'result': None} def _on_result(request_code, result_code, data): if request_code != _AZT_PICK_REQ_CODE: return if result_code == -1 and data is not None: path = data.getStringExtra('path') or '' result_holder['result'] = ( {'ok': True, 'path': path} if path else {'ok': False, 'error': 'no_path'}) else: result_holder['result'] = ( {'ok': False, 'error': 'cancelled'}) result_holder['done'] = True android_activity.bind(on_activity_result=_on_result)
PythonActivity.mActivity.startActivityForResult( intent, _AZT_PICK_REQ_CODE)
# Block-wait on the result. Caller is already on a # worker thread per the recorder_changes spec. import time while not result_holder['done']: time.sleep(0.05) return result_holder['result']
Subtleties — implementer to verify: - on_activity_result needs to fire on the Kivy/main thread; the worker thread waits on a Threading.Event instead of polling. - Multiple concurrent pick_project() calls should be prevented (Activity result routing is per-request- code; document that pick_project is single-flight). - If the server APK isn't installed: startActivityForResult throws ActivityNotFoundException → translate to {'ok': False, 'error': 'server_apk_not_installed'}, mirror what open_server_ui() already does for that case.
5. Recorder Android wiring: No recorder-side branch. The recorder calls pick_project() unconditionally; the client transport handles the desktop-vs-Android split. Recorder code is identical on both platforms.
Suite signing: the server APK and the recorder APK must already share /home/kentr/bin/azt-suite.keystore (per buildozer.spec line 66 in both apps). Without that match the Intent dispatch fails the signature-permission gate; that's the desired install-time failure, same gate the existing ContentProvider already relies on.
Verification (Android, in addition to desktop verification): - Install both APKs on a device. - Launch the recorder. Cold-start should bring the server APK's PickerActivity to the foreground via Intent. - Pick existing / open from disk / clone / start new — each should return through onActivityResult to the recorder, which then loads the chosen project. - Cancel the picker (system back) — recorder shows the "no project loaded" placeholder (cold start) or stays on the previous project (Start over). - Uninstall the server APK and try Start over — recorder surfaces "AZT collab service not installed" with the existing install-prompt flow that open_server_ui already uses.
design.cross_toolkit_proof:
New file: azt-collab/examples/non_kivy_pick.py
"""Demonstrates that a non-Kivy host can drive project selection via the standalone picker subprocess. No Kivy imports in this script.""" import subprocess import sys
def main(): proc = subprocess.run( [sys.executable, '-m', 'azt_collabd', 'projects'], capture_output=True, text=True) for line in (proc.stdout or '').splitlines(): if line.startswith('AZT_PICK\t'): _, path = line.split('\t', 1) print(f'picked: {path}') return 0 print(f'cancelled or failed (rc={proc.returncode})', file=sys.stderr) return 1
if __name__ == '__main__': sys.exit(main())
Also touch examples/sister_app.py (or add a comment) so the "subprocess pattern" example is discoverable.
design.concurrency:
Hard rule, set by the user: while the picker is open the recorder is paused. The user must not be able to use both at once. This applies on every platform.
Android — automatic via Activity lifecycle. When the PickerActivity in the server APK comes foreground via startActivityForResult, the recorder Activity is paused (onPause / onStop fires; Kivy stops dispatching frames). Nothing extra to wire.
Desktop — explicit, in pick_project()-on-the-recorder-side:
1. Before spawning the picker subprocess, the recorder's start_over() (and the cold-start path) calls self._auto_commit_sync() — the same hook go_picker() uses today (main.py:4538). Any unsynced recording, queue position, prefs change goes to disk and is committed/pushed before control transfers to the picker. If a sister app's commit/sync helper has a different name, document it in the host-contract docs.
2. Recorder shows a modal overlay ( kivy.uix.modalview.ModalView, auto_dismiss=False, label "Pick a project to continue.") that blocks input on the recorder window for the lifetime of the pick. The overlay is dismissed in _handle_pick (regardless of outcome — ok / cancelled / error).
3. Optional close-and-reopen: if the daemon turns out to have any cross-process locking complaint about the recorder holding a LIFT file open while the picker clones / creates from template, the recorder also releases its LIFTDatabase before pick_project() and re-loads it via load_lift on the way back. First-pass implementation: leave the file open. Switch to release-and-reload only if the smoke test surfaces a real conflict (the daemon already serializes git ops via azt_collabd/locks.py, so this is unlikely).
Implementer note: the modal overlay is recorder-side, not picker-side. The picker subprocess has its own UI; the overlay is purely to gray out the recorder window during the pick. Don't confuse the two.
design.icon_location:
Currently the suite icon lives at azt_collab_client/azt.png. Both standalone Kivy apps (settings UI + picker app) reference it via `os.path.dirname(azt_collab_client.__file__) + '/azt.png'`.
Open question: move to azt_collabd/azt.png (since the only consumers are daemon-side Kivy apps), or leave in azt_collab_client (where it accidentally also serves as a default icon for sister apps that don't set their own)?
Provisional: leave at azt_collab_client/. Reasoning: a sister app launching `python -m azt_collabd projects` already has azt_collab_client on its path; relying on that import works without any extra discovery. If a future sister app wants to override, it sets `app.icon` to its own path before mounting the picker.

step7_picker_entrypoint.verification:
From /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt_recorder/:
Picker subprocess in isolation: env/bin/python -m azt_collabd projects - Kivy window opens, lists existing projects (via daemon list_projects). Three create buttons present: "I have one on my phone" / "Clone Internet Repository" / "Start New". - Tap an existing project: window closes; stdout contains exactly one line starting with 'AZT_PICK\t'; exit 0. - Tap "I have one on my phone" → file chooser → pick a .lift → window closes; AZT_PICK line for that path; exit 0. Daemon registry now lists it (verify with a second `env/bin/python -m azt_collabd projects` invocation). - Tap "Clone Internet Repository" → URL prompt → submit → daemon clones → window closes; AZT_PICK line for the cloned lift_path; exit 0. - Tap "Start New" → langpicker → pick a language → daemon creates from template → window closes; AZT_PICK line for the new project; exit 0. - Close window without picking from any flow: exit 1; stdout has no AZT_PICK line. - Errors during clone/template (bad URL, no network): error label flashes on picker; window stays open; user can retry or close (exit 1).
Client API: env/bin/python -c "from azt_collab_client import pick_project; \ print(pick_project())" - Returns {'ok': True, 'path': '...'} on any successful flow above; the helper doesn't need to know which one.
Recorder UI (cold start): - Wipe last_lift from prefs; launch recorder. - Recorder window appears empty / placeholder; picker window appears in front. - Pick a project (any flow): picker closes; recorder loads the chosen LIFT; recording UI is ready. - Alternative: cancel picker on cold start → recorder stays in placeholder state; "Pick a project" button visible.
Recorder UI (Start over with project loaded): - Tap settings → Start over → picker window opens (separate process). Recorder window stays where it was. - Pick any project → picker closes → recorder switches to new LIFT. - Cancel picker → recorder stays on old project; no error log line written.
Cross-toolkit smoke: env/bin/python /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt-collab/examples/non_kivy_pick.py - Prints chosen path with no Kivy imports in the driver script.
Settings helper subprocess: env/bin/python -m azt_collabd ui - Window has credentials/host screens only. No Projects button in settings NavBar.
Recorder code-size check: - `wc -l main.py` significantly smaller than before (several hundred lines removed: open_file, clone_dialog, show_start_over, new_from_template, _download_and_open, _pending_vernlang plumbing, picker/langpicker KV + registration).
Cross-check both copies of azt_collab_client/ remain identical after the edits (recorder's symlink should make this automatic; diff at the end anyway).
step7_picker_entrypoint.do_not: - Do NOT mix Kivy log output into stdout in the picker app. Configure Kivy to log to stderr; reserve stdout for the AZT_PICK line. Test with stdout redirected to a file as well as a TTY. - Do NOT introduce a second in-process picker as a "Kivy fast path." One picker, always via pick_project(). On every platform. - Do NOT let the user interact with the recorder while the picker is open. Modal overlay during the pick on desktop; Activity lifecycle handles it on Android. See <concurrency>. - Do NOT block the recorder's Kivy main loop on subprocess.communicate(). pick_project() runs on a worker thread; the load_lift call comes back to the main loop via Clock.schedule_once. - Do NOT have the recorder import azt_collabd directly to spawn the picker. It calls pick_project() from azt_collab_client. azt_collab_client owns the spawn details; the recorder stays toolkit-agnostic-ready. - Do NOT change the project registry on-disk format. - Do NOT add a third entry point for "settings + projects combined." If a host wants both, it calls open_server_ui() and pick_project() separately. Two narrowly-scoped helpers beat one mega-app. - Do NOT delete go_picker() until you've grepped for every caller (recorder, tests/, examples/) and confirmed they all route through start_over() or are themselves removed.
step7_picker_entrypoint.resolved_questions:
All four open questions resolved 2026-05-01 by user decisions during the spec drafting session. Recorded here so an implementer doesn't try to relitigate them.
R1. Android sequencing — Option 1 (BUNDLE). Server-APK PickerActivity ships in step 7; recorder's in-process picker is deleted unconditionally. See <android>.
R2. Concurrency — recorder MUST background while picker is open; user MUST NOT be able to interact with both simultaneously. Recorder explicitly commits/syncs before launching the picker; treats the active project as released for the duration of the pick. See <concurrency> subsection in <design>.
R3. Cold-start UX — recorder window does NOT appear behind the picker. Pick first; bring up the recorder only after a project is loaded. Cancel on cold start = exit. Cancel on Start over = keep previous project. See cold- start sequencing in <recorder_changes>.
R4. Picker error display — modal overlay inside the picker window (a kivy.uix.modalview.ModalView with a Label and a Dismiss button). Window stays open after dismiss so the user can retry. Replaces the _show_error stub in PickerApp.
step7_picker_entrypoint.versioning: azt_collabd: 0.9.x → 0.10.0 (new entry point) azt_collab_client: 0.12.x → 0.13.0 (new pick_project helper) azt_recorder: 1.27.x → 1.28.0 (Start over routes through pick_project) Update CHANGELOG.txt (recorder), CHANGELOG.md (azt-collab), claud_diffs.txt (recorder), setup_from_nuke.sh per the global ground rules.

task.ground_rules: - User's CLAUDE.md says: increment version + update changelog for changes; output explicit diffs to claud_diffs.txt with brief rationale for each change; update setup_from_nuke.sh with each step. Apply these to every commit. - Sandboxing: stay inside /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt_recorder/ unless a change explicitly targets /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt-collab/. Both are in scope for this task. - Build debug rule: limit build output to relevant errors; don't read whole logs into context. - Today's date: 2026-04-30.
task.verification_checklist:
After each step, run from /home/kentr/bin/AZT/azt_recorder/: env/bin/python -c "import azt_collab_client; print(azt_collab_client.__version__)" env/bin/python -c "from azt_collab_client import open_server_ui; print(open_server_ui())" bash tests/step15.sh # smoke test for shared modules bash tests/step16.sh # sister-app example env/bin/python main.py # launch the recorder, exercise the welcome # → picker / langpicker / loaded recorder # path that the step touched.
Cross-check both copies of azt_collab_client/ remain identical (recorder copy and azt-collab copy). Diff them at the end of every step.
